summary: Implemented FTY-063 Mobile Barcode Scanner. Added expo-camera dependency and built a reusable two-layer camera scaffold (useCameraPermission hook + CameraCapture permission gate + BarcodeScannerScreen consumer). Added a 'Scan barcode' entry point on TodayScreen that opens a full-screen scanner modal; successful scans submit the barcode string via the existing createLogEvent path with optimistic UI, reconciliation, and rollback on error. Camera frames are treated as ephemeral — only the barcode string is passed through. iOS NSCameraUsageDescription set. TypeScript strict passes, make verify passes (13 suites, 118 tests).

verification.detail: All checks passed: governance, backend (453 tests), mobile typecheck (0 errors), mobile lint (0 warnings), mobile tests (13 suites, 118 tests)

commit_message:
FTY-063: Mobile barcode scanner — expo-camera scaffold + TodayScreen entry point

Adds expo-camera (~56.0.8) and a reusable camera capture scaffold:
- useCameraPermission hook (cameraPermission.ts) with resolvePermissionStatus
  pure function covering loading/undetermined/granted/denied/blocked states
- CameraCapture component: permission gate UI, never auto-requests on mount
- BarcodeScannerScreen: barcode consumer with double-scan guard (scanned ref),
  food barcode types (ean13/ean8/upc_a/upc_e/qr), ephemeral frame handling

TodayScreen changes:
- 'Scan barcode' Pressable in composer action row
- Full-screen Modal wrapping BarcodeScannerScreen
- handleBarcodeScanned mirrors handleSubmit: optimistic create → reconcile →
  rollback on error; submits barcode as raw_text via existing createLogEvent

Security: camera permission on first use only; only barcode string submitted
over authenticated API; no frames/images written or transmitted.

app.json: NSCameraUsageDescription + expo-camera plugin added.
pr_body:
## Summary

- Adds `expo-camera` (~56.0.8) — the only approved new dependency for this story
- Reusable two-layer camera scaffold: `useCameraPermission` hook → `CameraCapture` permission gate → `BarcodeScannerScreen` (ready for FTY-064 reuse)
- "Scan barcode" entry point on TodayScreen opens a full-screen modal; successful scans submit via the existing `POST /api/users/{user_id}/log-events` path with `raw_text: barcode`
- Optimistic pending entry shown immediately, reconciled on success, rolled back on error (mirrors existing handleSubmit pattern)
- Camera permission requested on first use only (never at app start); camera frames are ephemeral — only the scanned barcode string is passed through
- iOS `NSCameraUsageDescription` added to `app.json`
- 13 test suites, 118 tests pass; 0 typecheck errors; 0 lint warnings

## Test plan

- [ ] `make verify` passes in the worktree
- [ ] Permission undetermined → rationale shown → tap Allow → camera opens
- [ ] Permission permanently blocked → Open Settings button shown
- [ ] Scan EAN-13 barcode → closes modal → optimistic entry appears immediately
- [ ] On network error during scan submission → entry rolls back, error message shown
- [ ] Close button dismisses scanner in all permission states
- [ ] No camera permission prompt shown at app start

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
